title: geigeria pectidea
intro: Geigeria pectidea, also known as the "Yellow Flower", is a plant species within the Asteraceae family, commonly found in southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Geigeria pectidea has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues. The plant's herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be made from its leaves, roots, and flowers.
